jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Classic Simpson opening sequence
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated with character - extremely quotable but routine at this point

jokes.text: The only decent new one is Radiation Dude. - He's a cheap imitation of Radioactive Man.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy defending old superheroes
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by praising a knockoff
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven contradiction that sets up Comic Book Guy's pretentious hypocrisy

jokes.text: Up and Atom! With 'at 'em' spelled A-T-O-M, in a delicious pun.
jokes.setup: Explaining Radioactive Man's catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pretentious delivery of 'delicious pun'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay enhanced by Comic Book Guy's pompous appreciation of his own explanation

jokes.text: While Radiation Dude has a similar but lamer catchphrase, 'Up and let's go!'
jokes.setup: Building anticipation for the knockoff's catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Hilariously bland and unimaginative alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - the lameness of the knockoff catchphrase is the entire joke

jokes.text: We both have a special, limited-edition issue where he and Fallout Boy get killed on every page.
jokes.setup: Bragging about rare comic collection
jokes.punchline: The 'special edition' is just repetitive character death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor combined with absurd comic book logic - kids casually discussing repeated character death

jokes.text: I have got to do something about that air conditioner suction.
jokes.setup: Serious discussion about Radioactive Man movie
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy distracted by mundane air conditioning problem
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag that provides character moment but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: Need know star R.M. pic.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy using internet to find movie information
jokes.punchline: Abbreviated typing style contrasts with his verbose speech
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - verbose Comic Book Guy forced into internet brevity

jokes.text: I keep telling you, he's 73 years old and he's dead.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to bring back original Radioactive Man actor
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery of two insurmountable obstacles
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from age concern to death - deadpan delivery sells it

jokes.text: Granted, but-
jokes.setup: Death mentioned as obstacle to casting
jokes.punchline: Director still tries to argue the point
jokes.explanation: Brief but effective character beat showing Hollywood persistence even when facing death

jokes.text: Billowing backpacks, Radioactive Man! It's the worst villain of them all! The Scoutmaster!
jokes.setup: Flashback to campy 70s superhero show
jokes.punchline: The Scoutmaster as ultimate villain with 'billowing backpacks' as catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd 70s camp parody - 'billowing backpacks' as exclamation is inspired nonsense

jokes.text: Don't be afraid to use your nails, boys!
jokes.setup: Villain commanding his minions in battle
jokes.punchline: Encourages scouts to use fingernails as weapons
jokes.explanation: Dark humor masked as children's programming - using nails as weapons is hilariously inappropriate

jokes.text: This place must be hot. They don't need a big ad or even correct spelling.
jokes.setup: Looking at Springfield advertisement
jokes.punchline: Interpreting poor quality as sign of popularity
jokes.explanation: Hollywood logic perfectly inverted - bad marketing seen as sign of quality

jokes.text: We'll blow up our dams, destroy forests- anything! If there's a species of animal that's causing you problems, we'll have it wiped out!
jokes.setup: Quimby trying to attract Hollywood movie production
jokes.punchline: Escalates from property destruction to genocide
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Quimby's corrupt eagerness - ends on darkly absurd species extinction offer

jokes.text: Look, we just want to make movies, not kill things. Right. We understand.
jokes.setup: Producer's reasonable response to genocide offer
jokes.punchline: Quimby's 'understanding' suggests he missed the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Quimby's response suggests he fundamentally doesn't understand the objection

jokes.text: One of your favorite comic book heroes, 'Radio Man'- Radioactive Man, stupid! Strange. I shouldn't have been able to hear that.
jokes.setup: Skinner mispronounces character name
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges he heard a correction he shouldn't have been able to hear
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke breaking fourth wall - character acknowledges impossible hearing

jokes.text: Oh, and the air conditioner will be fixed this afternoon.
jokes.setup: End of important announcement about movie
jokes.punchline: Addresses minor earlier complaint as equally important
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier air conditioner gag - shows Skinner's administrative priorities

jokes.text: Marge, do you have other men in this house? Radioactive men?
jokes.setup: Homer hearing Bart practice Fallout Boy dialogue
jokes.punchline: Suspects infidelity specifically with radioactive men
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - jumps to absurd specific conclusion about radioactive adultery

jokes.text: If I get his role, I could finally come to terms with this funny little muddle called Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining motivation for wanting acting role
jokes.punchline: Pretentious self-analysis referring to himself in third person
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's occasional pretentiousness and self-awareness

jokes.text: Ev-er-y-one is... talk-ing a-bout... Ra-di-o-ac-tive M-an... y'all.
jokes.setup: Grampa attempting to be cool and current
jokes.punchline: Awkward rhythm and inappropriate 'y'all' addition
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa trying to be hip - the broken rhythm and random 'y'all' are perfectly awkward

jokes.text: Which one were you? The ugly one? - Were you the ugly one?
jokes.setup: Asking about Grampa's Little Rascals role
jokes.punchline: Both kids immediately assume he was 'the ugly one'
jokes.explanation: Kids' innocent cruelty perfectly delivered - they both assume the same unflattering role

jokes.text: No, I was the tough kid. Smelly. My shtick was lookin' into an exhaust pipe and gettin' a face full of soot.
jokes.setup: Grampa defending his Little Rascals role
jokes.punchline: Character named 'Smelly' whose act was getting dirty from exhaust pipes
jokes.explanation: Perfect old Hollywood absurdity - character name and repetitive schtick are perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: William Faulkner can write an exhaust pipe gag that would really make you think.
jokes.setup: Explaining the sophistication of old comedy writing
jokes.punchline: Claims literary genius Faulkner could elevate slapstick
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of high and low culture - imagining Faulkner writing slapstick is inspired absurdity

jokes.text: Oh, no! My favorite aggie! - You stole my bit! That's my bit!
jokes.setup: Grampa accidentally recreates his old slapstick routine
jokes.punchline: Accused of stealing his own decades-old bit
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Grampa accidentally performs his old act and gets accused of plagiarizing himself

jokes.text: He's killed the original Alfalfa! Yeah. Luckily, Alfalfa was an orphan, owned by the studio.
jokes.setup: Shocking announcement of Alfalfa's death
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that studio ownership makes it acceptable
jokes.explanation: Dark Hollywood satire - treating child actor death as property issue is brilliantly cynical

jokes.text: With wealthy Hollywood people on the way, local merchants can be forgiven for raising their prices a little.
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining Springfield's preparation for Hollywood
jokes.punchline: Understated description of obvious price gouging
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about local exploitation - understated delivery sells the cynicism

jokes.text: It's all in the delivery. 'Now is the winter of our discontent'-
jokes.setup: Milhouse giving acting advice
jokes.punchline: Quotes Shakespeare inappropriately to demonstrate 'delivery'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Milhouse's pretentiousness and misunderstanding of acting

jokes.text: No wonder no one came to my birthday party.
jokes.setup: Ralph failing at audition
jokes.punchline: Connects audition failure to broader social rejection
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph non-sequitur connecting unrelated failures - economical and character-perfect

jokes.text: What's for lunch tomorrow? - Chicken necks?
jokes.setup: Child supposed to respond to Radioactive Man
jokes.punchline: Completely ignores scene to ask about lunch menu
jokes.explanation: Good child logic - more concerned with lunch than superhero role-playing

jokes.text: That child has the exact qualities we're looking for! He's perfect! - I don't know. He didn't sign up officially. Oh, forget him then. It wouldn't be fair to the other children.
jokes.setup: Director finds perfect child actor
jokes.punchline: Bureaucracy prevents casting the ideal candidate
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of bureaucratic thinking prioritizing process over results

jokes.text: Congratulations, Bart Simpson. You're our new Fallout Boy! That's what I'd be saying to you if you weren't an inch too short. Next!
jokes.setup: Director appears to cast Bart in the role
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal due to height requirement
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds up expectation then crashes it with arbitrary requirement

jokes.text: Pull, you mighty stallions, pull! Show me no mercy!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to grow taller overnight
jokes.punchline: Dramatic dialogue while being stretched by horses
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with Bart's characteristic over-the-top approach to solving problems

jokes.text: Growing half an inch in one day is still pretty good, Bart. Yeah, that's how fast Grampa's shrinking.
jokes.setup: Lisa consoling Bart about partial growth success
jokes.punchline: Bart compares his growth to Grampa's aging decline
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about aging delivered with Bart's casual cruelty - good economy

jokes.text: Good news, gentlemen. I've grown that extra inch you wanted. Plus several feet more.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming to have grown the required inch
jokes.punchline: Obviously wearing platform shoes or stilts
jokes.explanation: Predictable visual gag but fits Bart's character - obvious solution executed with confidence

jokes.text: Out of the way. Milhouse, baby! Lionel Hutz, your new agent, bodyguard, unauthorized biographer and drug dealer- uh, keeper-awayer.
jokes.setup: Hutz introducing himself to new child star
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits to being drug dealer, quickly covers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lionel Hutz character moment - corrupt lawyer accidentally revealing illegal activities

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I can't hear you, son. I'm wearing a Jacuzzi suit.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he can't hear Bart's concerns
jokes.punchline: Wearing a Jacuzzi suit prevents hearing
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Homer logic - Jacuzzi suit as hearing impediment is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: George Burns was right. Show business is a hideous bitch goddess.
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on missing out on stardom
jokes.punchline: 10-year-old quoting George Burns about show business
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's premature cynicism expressed through old Hollywood wisdom

jokes.text: Someone he can hurl whiskey bottles at when he's feeling low.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing friendship duties to a child star
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions alcoholic violence as normal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about celebrity friendship expectations - Lisa's innocent delivery sells the darkness

jokes.text: I can suck up to him like the religious people suck up to God!
jokes.setup: Bart planning how to befriend famous Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Compares celebrity worship to religious devotion
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation delivered with Bart's characteristic irreverence toward religion

jokes.text: You know, I never walk out of a movie, but, yuck!
jokes.setup: Homer recognizing director of bad movie
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest review delivered directly to director
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer tactlessness - perfect character moment of accidental cruelty

jokes.text: But I will hire you for Angry the Clown, Silly Sailor and Dr. Clownius.
jokes.setup: Director refusing Krusty the main clown role
jokes.punchline: Offers multiple increasingly ridiculous clown variants
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of clown character names - 'Dr. Clownius' is the perfect absurd peak

jokes.text: I won't let you down! Well, I might let you down on Silly Sailor.
jokes.setup: Krusty promising professional dedication
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines promise with specific exception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character beat - promises reliability then immediately shows unreliability

jokes.text: Up and atom. - Up and at them. - Up and atom! - Up and at them! - Better.
jokes.setup: Director correcting actor's pronunciation of catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Completely misses the atomic pun in 'Up and Atom'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier pun explanation - director ruins the wordplay by correcting it

jokes.text: No, I'm just Milhouse when he gets hurt. Ohh!
jokes.setup: Bart thinking he found real Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Stunt double explains his specific role and immediately demonstrates
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about stunt doubles with immediate physical demonstration

jokes.text: It sounds like more fun than it really is. Hey! I think I'm lying on a broken bottle!
jokes.setup: Milhouse complaining about being crushed by truck
jokes.punchline: Discovers additional injury mid-complaint
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Milhouse's suffering - timing of bottle discovery adds to the misery

jokes.text: Beautiful! Use it.
jokes.setup: Milhouse discovers he's lying on broken glass
jokes.punchline: Director treats real injury as perfect acting
jokes.explanation: Dark Hollywood satire - director prioritizing authenticity over child safety, perfect economy

jokes.text: Good question! We'll check into that.
jokes.setup: Milhouse asking if X-rays are real
jokes.punchline: Director admits they don't know but will investigate later
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about film safety - casual attitude toward potentially harmful radiation

jokes.text: Cows don't look like cows on film. You gotta use horses. - What do you do if you want something that looks like a horse? - Usually we just tape a bunch of cats together.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they don't use real cows in movies
jokes.punchline: Escalates to taping cats together for horses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - logical progression leads to completely insane conclusion about cat-horses

jokes.text: Oh, I always wanted to be a Teamster. So lazy and surly. Mind if I relax next to ya?
jokes.setup: Homer meeting film crew workers
jokes.punchline: Admires their laziness and asks to join
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - openly admires negative qualities and wants to emulate them

jokes.text: Only the city has just passed another tax on puffy directing pants. - But I don't wear puffy pants! - I meant a tax on not wearing puffy pants.
jokes.setup: Quimby announcing new tax on directors
jokes.punchline: Adjusts tax to catch director either way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Quimby corruption - creates no-win tax situation, excellent escalation structure

jokes.text: But I don't wear puffy pants! I meant a, uh, tax on not wearing puffy pants.
jokes.setup: Tax on puffy directing pants
jokes.punchline: Immediately inverting the tax when it doesn't apply
jokes.explanation: Great bureaucratic absurdity with Quimby's instant policy reversal to maintain revenue

jokes.text: I like the way the blush brings out my cheekbones, but it's not worth it.
jokes.setup: Milhouse complaining about early makeup calls
jokes.punchline: Admits he likes how he looks in makeup
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Milhouse's vanity despite complaints about stardom

jokes.text: I've said 'jiminy jillickers' so many times, the words have lost all meaning.
jokes.setup: Milhouse describing the tedium of filming
jokes.punchline: Specific complaint about catchphrase repetition
jokes.explanation: Good meta commentary on repetitive filming - 'jiminy jillickers' is perfect innocent catchphrase

jokes.text: Again and again! And again and again and again!
jokes.setup: Director explaining need to reshoot from different angles
jokes.punchline: Increasingly manic repetition mirrors the filming process
jokes.explanation: Functional repetition gag but not particularly clever - mirrors the repetitive theme

jokes.text: Jiminy jillickers! - There, now, there's no need for profanity, Fallout Boy.
jokes.setup: Milhouse saying his innocent catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Radioactive Man treats it as profanity
jokes.explanation: Good irony - perfectly innocent phrase treated as curse word, callback to earlier meaninglessness

jokes.text: That Milhouse is going to be big! Gabby Hayes big!
jokes.setup: Homer predicting Milhouse's success
jokes.punchline: References obscure Western sidekick actor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's outdated reference shows his limited cultural knowledge

jokes.text: Now, that's real acid, so I want to see goggles, people.
jokes.setup: Director explaining dangerous stunt with acid
jokes.punchline: More concerned with crew safety than child actor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about film safety priorities - protects crew but not the child star

jokes.text: My eyes! The goggles do nothing!
jokes.setup: Radioactive Man actor exposed to acid
jokes.punchline: Discovers safety equipment is ineffective
jokes.explanation: Instant classic line - perfect economy and became hugely quotable meme

jokes.text: My eyes. The goggles do nothing.
jokes.setup: Acid scene gone wrong
jokes.punchline: Calm statement about safety equipment failure
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons quote - perfect deadpan delivery of equipment failure in crisis, became cultural touchstone

jokes.text: Where's the producer? I want to talk about this coffee.
jokes.setup: Expensive shot ruined, chaos on set
jokes.punchline: Actor focused on coffee quality amid disaster
jokes.explanation: Good actor priorities joke - completely misses the scale of the disaster

jokes.text: Jiminy jillickers, Radioactive Man! - Yes.
jokes.setup: Using old footage to complete film without Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Obviously mismatched dialogue and responses
jokes.explanation: Standard editing humor - obvious mismatch between question and response

jokes.text: Seamless, huh? - You're fired.
jokes.setup: Director proud of obviously bad editing work
jokes.punchline: Immediate firing for incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good economy in the firing - director's delusion met with swift consequence

jokes.text: But will they just find Milhouse, or will they find him and kill him? - Well, they'll- When they find him, they'll, um- - Excuse me, you didn't answer me. You just trailed off.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about search dogs' intentions
jokes.punchline: Handler can't answer and gets called out for evasion
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Bart's direct question exposing the handler's uncomfortable truth

jokes.text: Yeah. Yeah, I did kinda trail off there, didn't I?
jokes.setup: Being called out for trailing off
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the evasion technique
jokes.explanation: Mild meta humor acknowledging the conversational technique but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Did you know that there's a direct correlation between the decline of Spirograph and the rise in gang activity? Think about it. - I will. - No, you won't.
jokes.setup: Frink making absurd correlation claim
jokes.punchline: Correctly predicts Bart will ignore the suggestion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Frink absurdity with perfect character knowledge - he knows Bart won't actually think about it

jokes.text: The real heroes are out there, toiling day and night on more important things. - Television? - No! Curing heart disease and wiping out world hunger.
jokes.setup: Milhouse describing real heroes doing important work
jokes.punchline: Bart assumes he means television
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's entertainment-focused worldview

jokes.text: But, Milhouse, they haven't cured anything. Heart disease and world hunger are still rampant. Those do-gooders are all a bunch of pitiful losers.
jokes.setup: Milhouse praising disease researchers and hunger fighters
jokes.punchline: Bart dismisses them as failures due to ongoing problems
jokes.explanation: Dark but logical Bart reasoning - judges success purely by results rather than effort

jokes.text: You have to go to the Schwarzeneggers, the Stallones and to a lesser extent, the Van Dammes.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining who gets real results
jokes.punchline: Action stars ranked by effectiveness, Van Damme lowest
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect 90s action star hierarchy - Van Damme placement is perfectly insulting

jokes.text: Wow! Spanning two decades.
jokes.setup: Mickey Rooney claiming to be top draw 1939-1940
jokes.punchline: Milhouse impressed by two consecutive years
jokes.explanation: Good mathematical joke - Milhouse misinterprets two consecutive years as spanning decades

jokes.text: Uh, they tapped your tree house phone.
jokes.setup: Question about how Mickey found them in tree house
jokes.punchline: Studio surveillance of children's tree house communication
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail - treating tree house like serious communication hub worthy of surveillance

jokes.text: The only thing in show business that's hollow is the music industry.
jokes.setup: Milhouse calling movie stardom hollow
jokes.punchline: Mickey deflects by attacking music industry
jokes.explanation: Decent industry satire - old Hollywood star defending his territory by attacking another

jokes.text: Jiminy jillickers. - We're shutting down production.
jokes.setup: Bart delivers Fallout Boy catchphrase as replacement
jokes.punchline: Single line delivery causes immediate production shutdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single line delivery demonstrates how bad the replacement is

jokes.text: Ah, there's a $1,000 'leaving town' tax.
jokes.setup: Film crew trying to leave Springfield
jokes.punchline: Quimby creates final exit tax
jokes.explanation: Perfect final Quimby corruption - one last money grab as they escape

jokes.text: Folks from a Hollywood where values are different. They weren't thinking about the money. They just wanted to tell a story.
jokes.setup: Marge defending the filmmakers
jokes.punchline: Completely misreads their obvious commercial motivations
jokes.explanation: Good Marge naivety - completely misunderstands Hollywood's commercial nature

jokes.text: Should we give them some of their money back? - No.
jokes.setup: Brief consideration of returning Hollywood's money
jokes.punchline: Immediate rejection of any conscience
jokes.explanation: Good economy showing Springfield's collective greed - brief consideration followed by firm rejection

jokes.text: Hurry, Mr. Rooney. We've got a disenchanted little girl in a Jell-O Pudding commercial. - I could play that.
jokes.setup: Agent rushing Mickey to next gig
jokes.punchline: Mickey eager to play little girl role
jokes.explanation: Good desperation humor - aging actor willing to play inappropriate role for work

jokes.text: Thank God we're back in Hollywood, where people treat each other right.
jokes.setup: Producer relieved to leave Springfield
jokes.punchline: Claims Hollywood treats people better after being exploited
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending - Hollywood claiming moral superiority after being clearly exploitative themselves
